fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Dakota Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to South Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity south dakota, how to transfer dietitian license to south dakota, dietitian compact south dakota, south dakota dietitian endorsement application

board.name: South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners (SDBMOE)
board.mailing_address:
South Dakota Department of Health
Board of Examiners for Dietetic and Nutrition Practice
600 East Capitol Avenue
Pierre, SD 57501-2536
board.physical_address:
101 N. Main Ave., Suite 301
Sioux Falls, SD 57104

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (expires May 31 of odd years)

license_types.title: Licensed Nutritionist / Dietitian
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics in South Dakota, regulated by the Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners. The state law primarily licenses under the 'Nutritionist' title but requires qualifications identical to dietetics.

steps.title: Meet Educational Requirements
steps.description: Hold a bachelor's or master's degree with a major in dietetics, human nutrition, or food and nutrition, or an equivalent course of study, from an accredited college or university.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Practice
steps.description: Complete a supervised dietetic practice experience approved by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND).

steps.title: Pass the Examination
steps.description: Pass the registration examination for dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Complete the Application Form
steps.description: Download and accurately complete the 'Initial Licensure Application' PDF from the South Dakota Department of Health's 'Forms & Applications' page.

steps.title: Gather Supporting Documentation and Fee
steps.description: Compile all required documents, including official transcripts, verification of supervised practice, CDR exam score verification, a copy of your CDR card, and the non-refundable application fee. Payments can be made by check or money order payable to 'South Dakota Department of Health'.

steps.title: Submit Application Package
steps.description: Mail the completed 'Initial Licensure Application' form and all supporting documentation with payment to the South Dakota Department of Health at the address provided on the form, or submit via email to DOH.Dietitians@state.sd.us.

documents.detail: Application submitted via the SDBMOE portal.
Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration. Usually submitted directly or verified online by the Board.

provisional_text: South Dakota regulations require passing the national examination before full licensure. Check directly with the SDBMOE regarding temporary permits while awaiting examination results.
reciprocity_text: Applicants licensed in another state may be granted reciprocity if they hold an active license in good standing and can verify their Commission on Dietetic Registration status, demonstrating compliance with South Dakota's requirements.

faqs.question: How much does a South Dakota dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $70.

faqs.question: When does the South Dakota license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: South Dakota dietitian licenses expire biennially on May 31st of odd-numbered years. The renewal fee is $100.

faqs.question: Why does South Dakota use the 'Nutritionist' title for Dietitians?
faqs.answer: South Dakota codified its practice act using the term 'Licensed Nutritionist' for professionals providing medical nutrition therapy, but the requirements parallel those for Registered Dietitians nationally.

faqs.question: Does South Dakota have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: South Dakota has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to South Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Applicants licensed in another state may be granted reciprocity if they hold an active license in good standing and can verify their Commission on Dietetic Registration status, demonstrating compliance with South Dakota's requirements.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in South Dakota
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian or Nutritionist license in South Dakota, download and complete the 'South Dakota Dietetic and Nutrition Practice Licensure Application (PDF)' from the Department of Health's website and submit it via email or mail.

official_links.portal_name: South Dakota Dietetic and Nutrition Practice Licensure Application (PDF)
official_links.registration_steps: To apply for initial licensure, download the 'Initial Licensure Application' PDF from the board's 'Forms & Applications' page. Complete the form electronically or by hand, gather all required supporting documentation, and submit the entire application package via email or postal mail to the South Dakota Department of Health.
